items.front: What does this order prescribe?
items.back: Air traffic control procedures and phraseology for people providing ATC services.

items.front: Who does this order apply to?
items.back: All ATO personnel and anyone using ATO directives.

items.front: Where can users find this order online?
items.back: On the FAA Air Traffic Plans and Publications website and the FAA Orders and Notices website.

items.front: Which order did JO 7110.65BB cancel?
items.back: FAA Order JO 7110.65AA and all changes to it.

items.front: Where are significant changes to this order identified?
items.back: On the Explanation of Changes pages.

items.front: What does the publication table's cutoff date for completion mean?
items.back: The deadline by which a proposed change must be fully coordinated and signed to make that publication cycle.

items.front: If an FAA facility has not received the order 30 days before the effective date, who must it notify?
items.back: Its service area office distribution officer.

items.front: What must a proposed procedural-change submission include?
items.back: A description of the recommended change and the proposed language to be used in the order.

items.front: If more than one interpretation of the language can be inferred, where must the request go?
items.back: In writing to the Director, Policy (AJV-P).

items.front: When may an LOA specify minima below the minima in this order?
items.back: Only when appropriate military authority has authorized reduced separation between military aircraft.

items.front: What can justify a waiver to this order?
items.back: Exceptional or unusual requirements that require procedural deviations or supplementary procedures.

items.front: Who owns and executes the ATO Safety Management System?
items.back: All employees at all levels of the ATO.

items.front: When this order references a non-ATO regional office, which FAA region should a facility contact?
items.back: The region where the facility is physically located, not the region of its service area office.

items.front: What is each organization responsible for when this order changes?
items.back: Viewing, downloading, and subscribing to receive FAA email notifications about the changes.

items.front: What does the word "will" mean in this order?
items.back: Futurity, not a requirement for applying a procedure.

items.front: What angular difference defines crossing courses?
items.back: Forty-five through 135 degrees inclusive.

items.front: What is a note used for in this order?
items.back: Statements of fact or prefatory or explanatory material related to directive text.

items.front: What do references do in this order?
items.back: They direct attention to an additional or supporting source of information.

items.front: What do bold vertical lines in the margin mark?
items.back: The location of substantive revisions; next to a title, they mean extensive changes to that chapter, section, or paragraph.

items.front: What does APREQ stand for?
items.back: Approval Request.
